reset_tests.description: Apply synchronous active-high reset on a rising edge and verify count becomes 0 regardless of prior state. First drive enable=1 for several cycles to move count away from 0, then assert reset=1 before the next posedge and check count=0 exactly after that posedge.
Verify reset is not asynchronous. Drive counter to a nonzero value, assert reset between rising edges, and check count does not change until the next posedge clk.
Verify reset priority over enable. With count at a nonzero value, drive reset=1 and enable=1 together across a rising edge and expect count=0, not count+1.
Check reset polarity. Hold reset=0 while toggling clock and confirm no forced clearing occurs; then assert reset=1 on a rising edge and confirm clearing does occur.
After reset deassertion, verify count remains 0 when enable=0 on the following rising edge, proving no unintended post-reset increment or stale state.

normal_tests.description: Basic increment sequence from 0 with enable continuously high. After a reset, apply enable=1 for several rising edges and expect count sequence 0,1,2,3,4 on successive post-edge observations.
Hold behavior with enable low. Start from a known nonzero count and apply multiple rising edges with enable=0, reset=0; count must remain constant.
Alternating enable pattern to verify correct increment-versus-hold decisions on each rising edge.
Verify registered output behavior by checking count is stable between clock edges and changes only immediately after the intended rising edge.

corner_case_tests.description: Overflow wrap-around at maximum value. Drive count to 15, then apply enable=1 with reset=0 on the next rising edge and expect count=0.
Boundary transition 14 to 15 followed by 15 to 0 to catch off-by-one errors and wrong width implementations.
All-zeros control boundary. With reset=0 and enable=0 over repeated cycles, verify no spontaneous changes from any sampled state including 0 and 15.
Simultaneous reset and enable at boundary state 15. Expect reset to win and produce 0, distinguishing priority bugs from ordinary wrap logic.
Wrong-edge sensitivity check. Toggle enable and sample around a falling edge; count must not change on negedge and must change only on the following posedge if enable remains asserted.

Near-edge control changes to expose setup/hold style issues in test intent and distinguish combinational or glitchy implementations. Change reset or enable shortly after a posedge and confirm no effect until the next rising edge.

sequence_tests.description: Full modulo-16 traversal with enable high. After reset, apply 16 consecutive rising edges with enable=1 and verify the exact sequence 0->1->2->...->15->0.

Mixed control sequence exercising hold, increment, reset, and priority behavior in one run: reset, increment 3 times, hold 2 cycles, increment to 5, assert reset+enable together, then increment again.
One-cycle-late/one-cycle-early detection sequence. Use pattern enable=1,0,1,1,0 across consecutive rising edges after reset and verify expected counts 1,1,2,3,3 exactly after each posedge.
Nonzero reset recovery sequence. Increment to 9, hold one cycle, reset on next posedge, hold one cycle, then increment twice; expected sequence around reset is 9,9,0,0,1,2.
Edge-discrimination sequence. Keep enable asserted across several falling edges and rising edges while sampling after every edge to prove state updates occur only on rising edges.

random_tests.description: Generate 100 random cycle-by-cycle control pairs for (reset, enable), with biased inclusion of corner cases: roughly 20% reset=1, 20% simultaneous reset=1 and enable=1, and the rest mixed enable activity. Maintain a software reference model of the 4-bit synchronous counter and compare count after every rising edge. Also insert random mid-cycle control toggles and intermediate samples to catch asynchronous-reset, combinational-update, stale-output, and wrong-edge bugs.

key_discriminators: Reset is synchronous active-high only; asserting reset between rising edges must not immediately change count.
If reset and enable are both high on a rising edge, reset has priority and count must become 0.
Count must update on posedge clk, not negedge and not continuously.
When enable is low and reset is low on a rising edge, count must hold exactly with no drift or stale delayed update.
Transition 14->15 and 15->0 must be exact, exposing off-by-one logic and wrong counter width.
Output count is registered state, so checks between clock edges should distinguish registered-output implementations from combinational-output bugs.
Long increment sequences should expose one-cycle-early or one-cycle-late behavior.
Boundary tests at count=15 separate correct wrap-around from saturation-at-15 mutants.
Reset polarity tests separate active-high from incorrect active-low implementations.
Mid-cycle stimulus changes separate synchronous behavior from asynchronous or glitch-prone implementations.
